last_plan.reasoning: Maria is a high-LTV, low-risk Gold driver (24 months, €12,500 LTV, 0.02 cancellation rate, zero valid complaints in 12 months) experiencing a recurring, system-driven problem rather than a one-off. The combination of (a) tier-matched financial recovery, (b) tenure-weighted goodwill, and (c) an operational queue fix matches the severity and frequency of the issue. Pure monetary compensation without the fast-track voucher would leave the underlying frustration unresolved; the voucher without compensation would feel dismissive. Together they address both the symptom and the cause.
evidence_summary: Three short fares at LHR in the last 30 days, including the 135-minute wait in T-1001
Two related support tickets: T-1001 (open, 2h LHR wait) and T-1007 (closed, recent follow-up)
Live call expressing frustration and a pattern of repeated incidents
Gold-tier driver with 24 months tenure, €12,500 lifetime value, 0.02 cancellation rate, and 0 confirmed valid complaints in 12 months

proposed_actions.description: Tier-matched baseline recovery for a Gold driver hit by an airport short fare, aligned with her loyalty status and documented pattern.
proposed_actions.reason: Recurring airport short-fare pattern at LHR for a high-value Gold driver

proposed_actions.description: High-Value Partner Goodwill credit framing recognition of tenure and reliability, not a payout for the complaint. Requires lead approval per goodwill policy.
proposed_actions.reason: Justified by 24-month tenure, €12,500 lifetime value, 0.02 cancellation rate, and zero confirmed valid complaints in 12 months

proposed_actions.description: One-time Airport Fast-Track Voucher to be used tomorrow at LHR, skipping the queue assignment that produced the short fares.
proposed_actions.reason: Addresses root cause (queue assignment) rather than only the symptom; gives her a tangible operational win and a reason to stay online

proposed_actions.description: Acknowledge and apologize on the call. Validate frustration explicitly and reference prior tickets T-1007 and T-1001 so the history is treated as connected.
proposed_actions.reason: Driver frustration requires explicit validation before any financial remedy lands

proposed_actions.description: Escalate T-1001, formally link T-1007, and flag the 3-short-fares-in-30-days pattern to the LHR queue-assignment operations team.
proposed_actions.reason: Pattern must be investigated at the operational level, not only resolved at the ticket level

proposed_actions.description: 48-hour follow-up to confirm credits and voucher usage, re-measure sentiment and airport_short_fare_count_30d, and close the loop with operations on the queue-logic investigation.
proposed_actions.reason: Retention outcome must be verified, not assumed

last_plan.manager_message: Maria is a 24-month Gold driver with €12,500 LTV and a 0.02 cancellation rate, currently online and in active frustration on a call. The signal is operational, not behavioral: three short fares at LHR in 30 days, an open T-1001 ticket (135 min LHR T5 wait), a closed-without-resolution T-1007, and her live frustration. Recommended package: £50 INC-002 (tier-matched airport short-fare recovery) + £75 INC-007 (high-value partner goodwill, requires lead approval) + Q-103 one-time LHR fast-track voucher for tomorrow. Total monetary value: £125. This addresses both the symptom (financial recovery) and the cause (queue-assignment fix). Escalation needed to Operations/Queue-Logic team and a Support team lead for the goodwill approval. 48-hour follow-up owned by Retention specialist to confirm wallet credits, voucher usage, sentiment shift, and pattern closure. Compliance approval is pending and not being performed here.
assumptions: The 2-hour wait referenced on the call corresponds to ticket T-1001 (135 min at LHR T5)
Driver is currently willing to remain on platform (status ONLINE at time of data snapshot)
Goodwill credit INC-007 (£75) is within the support agent's escalation authority or can be approved by an on-call lead during the call
Q-103 fast-track voucher is technically available for activation tomorrow and not already consumed
No additional context exists about a specific churn threat or deactivation request from Maria beyond expressed frustration
